subject: LU-7419 llog: lock new llog object creation

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 1:

if a write to llog finds it non-existing, it should create it in the same transaction. so in OSP, the racing transaction are ordered by writes naturally. if so, then why this change is needed?

Patch Set 1:

Alex,it appears there is a real race, even though I am not sure the patch will help it.

See how llog_cat_new_log frees lgh_hdr and then opens up a transaction if there was not one and then later tries to do llog_init_handle.

The race is such taht if another thread somewhere finds this log handle in between those two actions and does llog_init_handle - we'll meet exactly the asertion we are seeing in this ticket.

I don't see any synchronisation for llog_init_handle calls anywhere to protect against this.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

this isn't a race with create yet as create doesn't really race with anyone. it's that failed create didn't mark logheader properly and another thread was able to get it by llog_cat_new_log() ?
see what we can do at the end of llog_cat_new_log() - an error handling for the case of failed initialization.

Patch Set 1:

"this isn't a race with create yet as create doesn't really race with anyone. it's that failed create didn't mark logheader properly and another thread was able to get it by llog_cat_new_log() ? see what we can do at the end of llog_cat_new_log() - an error handling for the case of failed initialization."

loghandle is not being protected by any lock during creation. The point is that other threads needs to wait until the new llog is created, which is the reason I add the lock. And I also added the error handler in the fix of LU-7039, patch 16969

Patch Set 1:

"if a write to llog finds it non-existing, it should create it in the same transaction. so in OSP, the racing transaction are ordered by writes naturally. if so, then why this change is needed?"

No, this is not in the same transaction as writes, see http://review.whamcloud.com/16333 and LU-6846.

Patch Set 1:

hm, not protected? what about down_write_nested(&loghandle->lgh_lock..) in llog_cat_current_log() ?

Patch Set 1:

creation in a separate transaction looks like a hack.. I think we discussed that LOHA_EXIST should be set in dt_create() context, no in RPC's reply?

Patch Set 1:

"hm, not protected? what about down_write_nested(&loghandle->lgh_lock..) in llog_cat_current_log() ?"

No, this is from log_cat_declare_add_rec(), not llog_cat_add_rec(). see the trace in LU-7419

Patch Set 1:

which shouldn't be doing creation ? :)

Patch Set 1:

"creation in a separate transaction looks like a hack.. I think we discussed that LOHA_EXIST"

I am not sure what you mean "hack", I thought we have been discuss creating remote llog object, and agree to do this, which can avoid a lot recovery trouble.

Patch Set 1:

"which shouldn't be doing creation ? :)"

No, this help us resolve recovery issue. I think this is roughly your proposal initially.

Patch Set 1:

what kind of recovery issue? I remember that we need to create a local reference to a remote llog synchronously otherwise we are not able to find that after failure. but this is a part of DNE initialization,  this is not something we need to do with every plain llog.

Patch Set 1:

"what kind of recovery issue? I remember that we need to create a local reference to a remote llog synchronously otherwise we are not able to find that after failure. but this is a part of DNE initialization, this is not something we need to do with every plain llog."

Because the following llog adding depends on the plain llog creation. If you put it in the same transaction, creation will only put it into the RPC, but the following operation will assume the plain llog has been created, then it might goes to the remote OUT before the creation transaction which will cause problem. Note: we only serializing adding the update records to the plain llog, not the whole process.

Patch Set 1:

how it can be if it's a single transaction? sorry, I still don't follow..

Patch Set 1:

"how it can be if it's a single transaction? sorry, I still don't follow.."

hmm, I mean if we put plain llog creation in the same transaction with adding record... . Actually I am not sure I follow your question ?

Patch Set 1:

yes, if we put dt_create() and llog_write() into a same transaction (like we do today for the regular cases), then what's wrong?

Patch Set 1:

again, osp_md_create() should be setting LOHA_EXISTS, of course. it's not any different from OSD setting LOHA_EXISTS when we create an inode in memory?

Patch Set 1:

"again, osp_md_create() should be setting LOHA_EXISTS, of course. it's not any different from OSD setting LOHA_EXISTS when we create an inode in memory?"

It is not about OSP, it is to make sure llog_create arrives before other appending.  Right now, we only control the order for appending record in osp_md_write(), that is the reason.

Btw: I do not think LOHA_EXISTS is good enough for OSP object, I see a lot issue in racer and 24 hours failover test. But that is another question.

Patch Set 1:

well, but create is in the same transaction, it's followed by write and both are done under the exclusive mutex, correct? then automatically all subsequent appends should be ordered after that first transaction creating/initializing llog?
as for LOHA_EXISTS - I think it'd be very helpful to have a ticket describing the issue with the details. thanks in advance :)

Patch Set 1:

"well, but create is in the same transaction, it's followed by write and both are done under the exclusive mutex, correct? then automatically all subsequent appends should be ordered after that first transaction creating/initializing llog?"

I am afraid no, we only lock the plain llog, instead of the whole catlog during creation + appending.  


 as for LOHA_EXISTS - I think it'd be very helpful to have a ticket describing the issue with the details. thanks in advance :)

There is already aa ticket for this on racer LDEV-xx, sorry I forgot the number.

Patch Set 1:

at any time we can create only one plain llog, so there should be no concurrency at all?

Patch Set 1:

not sure I follow. You mean other threads has to wait until the llog object is created? how?

Patch Set 1:

btw: I think this remote llog thing add some uncertain here. there maybe better solution here. But the current create llog in declare indeed eliminate a lot LBUG on OUT in our 24 hours test. I really do not want to spend another month to dig into this to pursue another idea. Unless current approach has serious problem.

Patch Set 1:

llog_cat_current_log() returns plain llog with the mutex held, then llog_cat_add_rec() check is it need to be created. once created, we add a new record to the plain llog and then release the semaphore. another thread got this llog now, it's created and there is write in the same transaction.

Patch Set 1:

well, it sounds like instead of fixing a root cause (not that I really know this for sure), you fixed one user of that code. so the question then how long will it take for another user to hit the same issue?

Patch Set 1:

The other thread will use llog_exist() to check if the current llog is created,  which is not trustable. what if the previous creation failed.

Patch Set 1:

"well, it sounds like instead of fixing a root cause (not that I really know this for sure), you fixed one user of that code. so the question then how long will it take for another user to hit the same issue?"

what issue? I think creating the plain llog in declaration will save us a lot trouble to deal with the failure and recovery cases, why not do that?

Patch Set 1:

how it's not trustable? dt_create() was called and it was successful? yes, later RPC can fail, but it's not any different from ldiskfs which can fail to update disk with -EIO ?

Patch Set 1:

But OUT can not handle this gracefully.

Patch Set 1:

the first issue is that we've got two different paths: for local llog and for remote one - this is wrong. another issue is that there will be (or already exist) another use cases with similar behavior. so they will hit this issue too?

Patch Set 1:

"the first issue is that we've got two different paths: for local llog and for remote one - this is wrong. "

I admit this is not nice, but IMHO, we all agree current llog is not good enough for remote update llog. I thought this will be changed pretty soon. That is why I do not want to spend much time here, unless there are serious performance issue or difficult to understand ;).  Actually I thought the current approach is simple enough and easy to maintain. 

"another issue is that there will be (or already exist) another use cases with similar behavior. so they will hit this issue too"

What use cases? This is only used by update llog.

commit.subject: LU-7419 llog: lock new llog object creation
commit.message:
LU-7419 llog: lock new llog object creation

Lock the new llog object creation to avoid two
process create the same object at the same time.

Signed-off-by: Di Wang <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: Icdc0eec534ca2f15cd0e195df951416953195346
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17132
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Mike Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
